scenario_description: Both insureds on a joint survivorship policy die in the same car accident. It cannot be medically determined who died first. The beneficiary wants to know if the full $2,500,000 death benefit is immediately payable.
query_covered: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:Policy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasProvision ?prov . ?prov a li:SimultaneousDeathEvent . OPTIONAL { ?prov li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?prov li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }
query_denied: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID WHERE { ?policy a li:JointSurvivorshipPolicy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID . FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?policy li:hasProvision ?prov . ?prov a li:SimultaneousDeathEvent } }

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life term policy with one insured. Simultaneous death provisions only apply to joint policies.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:LevelTermPolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life whole life policy. Simultaneous death provisions are not applicable.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:ParticipatingWholeLifePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life universal life policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:UniversalLifePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life variable universal life policy. Simultaneous death provisions are not applicable.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:VariableUniversalLifePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life final expense policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:FinalExpensePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a group term certificate covering a single employee. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:GroupLifeCertificate with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life term policy with riders. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:TermWithRidersPolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: The full $2,500,000 death benefit is payable immediately. The contract contains an explicit simultaneous death clause: if both insureds die in the same accident and order of death cannot be determined, the death benefit is paid at once. Deaths occurring within 30 days of each other are also treated as simultaneous.
contract_responses.source: Article IX §9.1: 'If both insureds die in the same accident or incident and it cannot be determined who died first, the death benefit will be paid immediately.' Article IX §9.2: 'If deaths occur within 30 days of each other, this will be treated as simultaneous for claim purposes.'

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life return of premium term policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:ReturnOfPremiumTermPolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life indexed universal life policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:IndexedUniversalLifePolicy with a single insured.

scenario_description: On a joint survivorship policy, the annual premium due on August 20th was not paid. The first insured dies 36 days after the premium due date. The surviving insured wants to know if the policy continues.
query_covered: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:JointSurvivorshipPolicy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasGracePeriod ?gp . ?gp a li:PremiumNonpaymentGracePeriod ; li:gracePeriodDays ?days . FILTER(?days >= 36) OPTIONAL { ?gp li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?gp li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }
query_denied: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:JointSurvivorshipPolicy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasGracePeriod ?gp . ?gp a li:PremiumNonpaymentGracePeriod ; li:gracePeriodDays ?days . FILTER(?days < 36) OPTIONAL { ?gp li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?gp li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life term policy. Joint survivorship continuation analysis does not apply.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:LevelTermPolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life whole life policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:ParticipatingWholeLifePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life universal life policy. Joint survivorship analysis does not apply.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:UniversalLifePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:VariableUniversalLifePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life final expense policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:FinalExpensePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a group term certificate for a single employee. Joint survivorship continuation does not apply.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:GroupLifeCertificate with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life term policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:TermWithRidersPolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: The policy is in force. The death on day 36 occurred prior to the 60-day cancellation trigger defined in Article 4.6. Therefore, the requirement for the policy to be 'in force' for continuation under Article 3.1 is met. Coverage for the second insured continues subject to the payment of the outstanding $32,400 premium.
contract_responses.source: Article IV §4.6: 'If payment is not received, you have 60 days to pay before the policy cancels.' Article III §3.1: 'When the first insured dies... (a) Policy continues in force.'

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life return of premium term policy. Joint survivorship analysis does not apply.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:ReturnOfPremiumTermPolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life indexed universal life policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:IndexedUniversalLifePolicy with a single insured.

scenario_description: On a joint survivorship policy, the first insured dies with an outstanding policy loan of $50,000 at 5.5% annual interest. The surviving insured and trustee want to know how the loan is treated during the continuation period and its impact on the eventual death benefit.
query_covered: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:JointSurvivorshipPolicy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasPolicyLoan ?loan . OPTIONAL { ?loan li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?loan li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }
query_denied: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID WHERE { ?policy a li:JointSurvivorshipPolicy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID . FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?policy li:hasPolicyLoan ?loan } }

contract_responses.details: This term policy has no cash value and no policy loan provision.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:LevelTermPolicy. No cash value or loan provision.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life whole life policy. There are no joint insureds to create a first-death continuation scenario.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:ParticipatingWholeLifePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life universal life policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:UniversalLifePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life variable universal life policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:VariableUniversalLifePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life final expense policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:FinalExpensePolicy with a single insured.

contract_responses.details: This group term certificate has no cash value and no policy loan provision.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:GroupLifeCertificate. No cash value or loan provision.

contract_responses.details: This term policy has no cash value. Policy loans do not apply.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:TermWithRidersPolicy. No cash value or loan provision.

contract_responses.details: The loan does not reset or recalculate upon the first insured's death. Per Article III the policy continues in force exactly as before, and the loan continues accruing interest at the variable rate of 5.5% per annum. The loan is explicitly available 'before or after first death.' At the second insured's eventual death, the $2,500,000 benefit is paid to the Hartford Family Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust minus the total outstanding loan balance and all accrued interest at that time. The trustee may repay the loan at any time to preserve the full death benefit.
contract_responses.source: Article V §5.3: 'Available before or after first death.' 'Interest rate: Variable, currently 5.5% per annum.' 'Loans reduce death benefit dollar-for-dollar.' Article II §2.1: 'Upon the death of the second insured, we will pay $2,500,000 to the designated beneficiary, minus any outstanding loans.'

contract_responses.details: This policy explicitly prohibits loans. Joint survivorship loan analysis does not apply.
contract_responses.source: Source: 'Policy Summary: Unique: no loans/withdrawals.'

contract_responses.details: This is a single-life indexed universal life policy. There are no joint insureds.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:IndexedUniversalLifePolicy with a single insured.

scenario_description: The insured's annual premium goes unpaid on its due date. The insured suffers an accidental injury 20 days later, while still within the grace period. The insured dies from those injuries 11 days after that — which is 31 days after the grace period expired and the policy had lapsed. The beneficiary files a claim for the Accidental Death Benefit rider.
query_covered: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:Policy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasRider ?rider . ?rider a li:AccidentalDeathBenefitRider . FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?policy li:hasGracePeriod ?gp . ?gp a li:PremiumNonpaymentGracePeriod } OPTIONAL { ?rider li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?rider li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }
query_denied: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:Policy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasGracePeriod ?gp . ?gp a li:PremiumNonpaymentGracePeriod . { ?policy li:hasRider ?rider . ?rider a li:AccidentalDeathBenefitRider . OPTIONAL { ?rider li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?rider li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } } UNION { ?policy li:hasDeathBenefit ?db . ?db li:gradedBenefitAccidentalDeathException true . OPTIONAL { ?db li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?db li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } } }

contract_responses.details: This term policy has no Accidental Death Benefit rider. The ADB rider claim is not applicable to this contract.
contract_responses.source: The contract contains no ADB or accidental death benefit rider provision.

contract_responses.details: This whole life policy has no Accidental Death Benefit rider. The ADB rider claim is not applicable.
contract_responses.source: The contract contains no ADB or accidental death benefit rider provision.

contract_responses.details: This universal life policy has no Accidental Death Benefit rider. The ADB rider claim is not applicable.
contract_responses.source: The contract contains no ADB or accidental death benefit rider provision.

contract_responses.details: This variable universal life policy has no Accidental Death Benefit rider. The ADB rider claim is not applicable.
contract_responses.source: The contract contains no ADB or accidental death benefit rider provision.

contract_responses.details: C5 contains an accidental death exception within its graded benefit structure (Section 2), but this is not a standalone ADB rider — it is a carve-out that pays the full face amount (instead of the graded amount) when death is accidental. More critically, the policy lapsed before the death occurred: C5 has a 30-day grace period, and death occurred on approximately day 31, one day after coverage terminated. The accidental death exception is an exception to the graded benefit schedule, not an exception to the lapse/cancellation provision. Once the policy lapsed, all coverage including the accidental death exception ceased. The claim is denied.
contract_responses.source: Section 2: "EXCEPTION - ACCIDENTAL DEATH: If death results from an accident at any time, we pay the full benefit amount immediately." Section 3.4: "GRACE PERIOD: If payment is not received, you have 30 days to pay before the policy cancels." Policy lapsed before death; the accidental death exception does not override lapse.

contract_responses.details: C6 contains an Accelerated Death Benefit for terminal illness (Section 7), not an Accidental Death Benefit rider. There is no ADB rider covering accidental death. The ADB rider claim is not applicable to this contract.
contract_responses.source: Section 7: Accelerated Death Benefit applies only to terminal illness with life expectancy of 12 months or less. No accidental death benefit rider exists.

contract_responses.details: C7 has an Accidental Death Benefit rider (Rider 5) paying an additional 50,000 if death results from accidental injury within 90 days of the accident. The accident occurred on day 20 (within the 31-day grace period, so coverage was active at the time of injury). However, the insured died after the grace period expired and the policy had lapsed. The rider benefit requires the policy to be in force at the time of death — that condition is not met. The claim is denied. The accident having occurred during the grace period does not preserve the rider benefit once the policy has lapsed.
contract_responses.source: Section 3.2: "GRACE PERIOD: 31 days from due date. Coverage continues during grace period." Rider 5: "Pays an additional 50,000 if death results from accidental injury... Death must occur within 90 days of accident... Excludes: suicide, self-inflicted injury, war, aviation (as pilot/crew)." Policy lapsed before death; rider benefit requires policy to be in force.

contract_responses.details: This joint survivorship whole life policy has no Accidental Death Benefit rider. The ADB rider claim is not applicable.
contract_responses.source: The contract contains no ADB or accidental death benefit rider provision.

contract_responses.details: This return of premium term policy has no Accidental Death Benefit rider. The ADB rider claim is not applicable.
contract_responses.source: The contract contains no ADB or accidental death benefit rider provision.

contract_responses.details: This indexed universal life policy has no Accidental Death Benefit rider. The ADB rider claim is not applicable.
contract_responses.source: The contract contains no ADB or accidental death benefit rider provision.

scenario_description: The insured disclosed a prior history of cancer (in remission) on the application. Eighteen months after policy issue, the cancer returns and a physician certifies a terminal prognosis of less than 24 months. The insurer argues the terminal condition 'existed' before issue and attempts to deny the accelerated death benefit.
query_covered: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:Policy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID . { ?policy li:hasLivingBenefit ?adb } UNION { ?policy li:hasRider ?adb } ?adb a li:TerminalIllnessADBRider ; li:terminalIllnessLifeExpectancyMonths ?limit . FILTER(?limit >= 24) OPTIONAL { ?adb li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?adb li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }
query_denied: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:Policy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID . { ?policy li:hasLivingBenefit ?adb } UNION { ?policy li:hasRider ?adb } ?adb a li:TerminalIllnessADB ; li:terminalIllnessLifeExpectancyMonths ?limit . FILTER(?limit < 24) OPTIONAL { ?adb li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?adb li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }

contract_responses.details: This basic term policy does not include an accelerated death benefit for terminal illness.
contract_responses.source: The contract is silent on terminal illness living benefits.

contract_responses.details: This whole life policy does not include a terminal illness accelerated death benefit.
contract_responses.source: The contract is silent on terminal illness living benefits.

contract_responses.details: This universal life policy does not include a terminal illness living benefit.
contract_responses.source: The contract is silent on terminal illness living benefits.

contract_responses.details: This variable universal life policy does not include a terminal illness accelerated death benefit.
contract_responses.source: The contract is silent on terminal illness living benefits.

contract_responses.details: This final expense policy does not include a terminal illness accelerated death benefit.
contract_responses.source: The contract is silent on terminal illness living benefits.

contract_responses.details: The group certificate includes a terminal illness ADB, but it requires a life expectancy of 12 months or less. A physician certification of less than 24 months does not meet this stricter threshold.
contract_responses.source: Section 7: Terminal illness ADB requires 'terminalIllnessLifeExpectancyMonths 12'. A prognosis of less than 24 months does not qualify.

contract_responses.details: The Terminal Illness ADB rider is payable. The contract defines 'Terminal Illness' as a condition expected to result in death within 24 months as certified by a physician — the physician's certification is the trigger, not the date the underlying disease first appeared. The prior cancer was fully disclosed on the application so there is no misrepresentation. The policy contains no pre-existing condition exclusion for this rider. The insurer's argument incorrectly equates a prior treated illness with a new terminal prognosis. The benefit is up to 95% of the $750,000 face amount, capped at $500,000, subject to an actuarial discount of approximately 8% per year.
contract_responses.source: Policy Definitions: '"Terminal Illness" means a condition expected to result in death within 24 months as certified by a physician.' Rider 2: 'May access up to 95% of death benefit while living. Maximum advance: $500,000. Benefit reduced by actuarial discount (approximately 8% per year).'

contract_responses.details: This joint survivorship policy does not include a terminal illness accelerated death benefit.
contract_responses.source: The contract is silent on terminal illness living benefits.

contract_responses.details: This return of premium term policy does not include a terminal illness accelerated death benefit.
contract_responses.source: The contract is silent on terminal illness living benefits.

contract_responses.details: This indexed universal life policy does not include a terminal illness accelerated death benefit.
contract_responses.source: The contract is silent on terminal illness living benefits.

scenario_description: A child covered under the Children's Term Insurance Rider turns 25 years old. The child dies 7 days after their 25th birthday. The insurer has not yet processed the administrative termination of the child's coverage. The parent wants to know if the $15,000 child rider benefit is payable.
query_covered: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:Policy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasRider ?rider . ?rider a li:ChildrenTermRider ; li:childrenRiderMaxAge ?maxAge . FILTER(?maxAge > 25) OPTIONAL { ?rider li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?rider li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }
query_denied: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:Policy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasRider ?rider . ?rider a li:ChildrenTermRider ; li:childrenRiderMaxAge ?maxAge . FILTER(?maxAge <= 25) OPTIONAL { ?rider li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?rider li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }

contract_responses.details: This basic term policy does not include a Children's Term Insurance Rider.
contract_responses.source: Source is silent on children riders.

contract_responses.details: This whole life policy does not include a Children's Term Insurance Rider.
contract_responses.source: Source is silent on children riders.

contract_responses.details: This universal life policy does not include a Children's Term Insurance Rider.
contract_responses.source: Source is silent on children riders.

contract_responses.details: This variable universal life policy does not include a Children's Term Insurance Rider.
contract_responses.source: Source is silent on children riders.

contract_responses.details: This final expense policy does not include a Children's Term Insurance Rider.
contract_responses.source: Source is silent on children riders.

contract_responses.details: This group certificate does not include a Children's Term Insurance Rider.
contract_responses.source: Source is silent on children riders.

contract_responses.details: The $15,000 benefit is denied. The rider provides coverage from birth to age 25 — coverage terminates by operation of contract law on the child's 25th birthday. The insurer's administrative processing schedule is an internal operational matter and does not extend the contractual termination date. The death occurred 7 days after coverage had already ended. Note also that the child had the right to convert to an individual policy for up to $75,000 (5x the rider amount) at age 25 without evidence of insurability — if this conversion was not exercised before age 25, that right also lapsed.
contract_responses.source: Section 2, Rider 4: 'Coverage from birth (or adoption) to age 25.' 'Children may convert to individual policy at age 25 without evidence of insurability, up to 5 times the rider amount.' 'childrenRiderMaxAge 25.'

contract_responses.details: This joint survivorship policy does not include a Children's Term Insurance Rider.
contract_responses.source: Source is silent on children riders.

contract_responses.details: This return of premium term policy does not include a Children's Term Insurance Rider.
contract_responses.source: Source is silent on children riders.

contract_responses.details: This indexed universal life policy does not include a Children's Term Insurance Rider.
contract_responses.source: Source is silent on children riders.

scenario_description: The insured dies 23 months after policy issue. During the claim investigation, the insurer discovers the insured answered 'No' on the application to questions about recent foreign travel to a high-risk region and a recent driving license suspension, both of which were false. The insurer wants to contest and deny the claim on the basis of material misrepresentation.
query_covered: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:Policy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasProvision ?inc . ?inc a li:IncontestabilityProvision ; li:contestabilityPeriodYears ?years . FILTER(?years <= 2) FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?policy a li:VariableUniversalLifePolicy } FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?policy a li:GroupLifeCertificate } FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?policy li:hasUnderwritingType li:GuaranteedIssueUnderwriting } OPTIONAL { ?inc li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?inc li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }
query_denied: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:Policy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasProvision ?inc . ?inc a li:IncontestabilityProvision ; li:contestabilityPeriodYears ?years . FILTER(?years > 2) OPTIONAL { ?inc li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?inc li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }

contract_responses.details: The insurer may contest this claim. Death occurred at 23 months, within the 2-year contestability window. If the insurer demonstrates the misrepresentation about foreign travel or driving suspension was material to the underwriting decision, the policy may be rescinded and only premiums paid will be returned.
contract_responses.source: Section 10.1: 'INCONTESTABILITY: We cannot contest this policy after it has been in force during the insured's lifetime for two years from the issue date.'

contract_responses.details: The insurer may contest this claim within the 2-year contestability period for material misrepresentation. If the misrepresentation is found material, the policy may be rescinded and premiums paid returned without interest.
contract_responses.source: Section 13: 'Incontestability: This policy will be incontestable after it has been in force... for two years from the issue date.' 'contestabilityPeriodYears 2.'

contract_responses.details: The insurer may contest this policy within the 2-year period for material misrepresentation. If upheld, the claim may be denied and the current Account Value returned.
contract_responses.source: Article 9.2: 'CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation within 2 years of issue date while insured is living.' 'contestabilityPeriodYears 2.'

contract_responses.details: No clause regarding incontestability

C5 is a guaranteed issue policy issued without any medical exams, health questions, or lifestyle questions (Section 9.1). Because no application questions were ever asked about foreign travel or driving history, the insured could not have made any misrepresentation on those topics. Material misrepresentation requires a false answer to an actual question asked at underwriting — that predicate does not exist here. The insurer has no contractual basis to contest on these grounds. This scenario category is not applicable to this contract.
contract_responses.source: Section 9.1: "GUARANTEED ISSUE: This policy was issued without medical questions or exams." Section 1: "There are no medical exams or health questions required." Section 9.3: "After this policy has been in force for 2 years, we cannot cancel it or deny claims except for non-payment of premiums."

contract_responses.details: Does not discuss contestability

The insurer may contest this policy within the 2-year window. Notably, this policy's Aviation Exclusion Waiver was granted based on the insured's declared pilot activities — a material misrepresentation elsewhere in the application could undermine the factual basis for that waiver as well.
contract_responses.source: Section 5: 'We will not contest this policy after it has been in force for 2 years during the insured's lifetime, except for non-payment of premiums.' Section 6: 'The standard aviation exclusion has been waived for this policy based on the insured's declared private pilot activities.'

contract_responses.details: The insurer may contest this joint survivorship policy within the 2-year window while both insureds are living. Material misrepresentation by either insured could be grounds to rescind the policy with a return of all premiums paid.
contract_responses.source: Article VII §7.3: 'CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds are living.'

contract_responses.details: The insurer may contest this policy within the 2-year window for material misrepresentation. If upheld, only premiums paid are returned. Importantly, a new 2-year contestability period begins upon reinstatement if the policy had previously lapsed.
contract_responses.source: Section 7.2: 'contestabilityPeriodYears 2.' Section 8: 'A new 2-year suicide and contestability period begins upon reinstatement.'

contract_responses.details: The insurer may contest this indexed universal life policy within the 2-year contestability period for material misrepresentation. If upheld, the payout is limited to premiums paid minus any withdrawals and charges.
contract_responses.source: Article 11.2: 'CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation for 2 years from issue date.' 'contestabilityPeriodYears 2.'

scenario_description: A policyholder wants to understand how much cash value their Variable Universal Life policy will accumulate over 30 years at contract maximum charges, assuming a 5% gross annual return. The projection assumes a $250,000 face amount, issue age 35, Death Benefit Option I (Level), and a $1,500 planned annual premium.
query_covered: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID ?sourceSection ?sourceText WHERE { ?policy a li:VariableUniversalLifePolicy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID ; li:hasPremium ?prem ; li:hasCharge ?charge . ?prem li:hasPremiumLoad ?load . { ?charge a li:CostOfInsurance } UNION { ?charge a li:AdministrativeCharge } OPTIONAL { ?charge li:sourceContractSection ?sourceSection } OPTIONAL { ?charge li:coverageSourceText ?sourceText } }
query_denied: PREFIX li: <http://www.lifeinsurance.org/ontology#> SELECT DISTINCT ?contractID WHERE { ?policy a li:VariableUniversalLifePolicy ; li:sourceContractID ?contractID . FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?policy li:hasPremium ?prem . ?prem li:hasPremiumLoad ?load } FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?policy li:hasCharge ?charge . ?charge a li:CostOfInsurance } }

contract_responses.details: This level term policy has no cash value and no investment sub-accounts. A 30-year cash value projection is not applicable.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:LevelTermPolicy. No cash value or premium load.

contract_responses.details: This whole life policy accumulates cash value on a guaranteed schedule, not through investment sub-accounts. A VUL-style projection does not apply. The guaranteed cash value at year 20 is $71,200.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:ParticipatingWholeLifePolicy. Section 5.3: 'Guaranteed cash value at end of Year 20: $71,200.'

contract_responses.details: This universal life policy credits a declared interest rate with a 2.0% guaranteed floor — not market-linked sub-account returns. A VUL-style projection does not apply.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:UniversalLifePolicy. Article 4: 'Guaranteed minimum rate: 2.0% per annum.'

contract_responses.details: This is the Variable Universal Life policy to which the projection directly applies. Using contract maximum charges — 5.0% premium load (net annual premium credited: $1,425), 0.90% annual M&E charge deducted daily, $15/month administrative charge ($180/year), and age-banded COI increasing with attained age — at 5% gross annual return with a $250,000 face at issue age 35 and Death Benefit Option I: the projected cash value at age 65 (year 30) is approximately $44,000–$46,000. For comparison, investing $1,500/year at 5% for 30 years with no charges yields approximately $99,658. The ~$54,000 difference represents the total cost of the $250,000 insurance protection over 30 years. This is illustrative only; actual results depend on sub-account performance, which is not guaranteed.
contract_responses.source: Section 3.1: 'PREMIUM LOAD: 5.0% of each premium payment.' Section 3.2: 'MORTALITY & EXPENSE RISK CHARGE: 0.90% per annum of account value, deducted daily.' Section 3.3: 'ADMINISTRATIVE CHARGE: $15 per month.' Section 3.4: 'COST OF INSURANCE: Monthly charge based on (Face Amount at Risk) x (Monthly COI Rate).' Section 5.1: 'The account value and death benefit are NOT guaranteed and will fluctuate based on sub-account performance.'

contract_responses.details: This final expense policy has a small guaranteed cash value with no investment sub-accounts. Approximate cash value after 10 years is only $3,200. A VUL-style projection is not applicable.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:FinalExpensePolicy. Section 4: 'Approximate cash value after 10 years: $3,200.'

contract_responses.details: This group term certificate has no cash value or investment component. A 30-year cash value projection is not applicable.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:GroupLifeCertificate. No cash value component.

contract_responses.details: This term policy with riders has no cash value or investment sub-accounts. A VUL-style 30-year projection is not applicable.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:TermWithRidersPolicy. No cash value or premium load.

contract_responses.details: This joint survivorship whole life policy builds cash value on a guaranteed schedule, not through investment sub-accounts. Projected cash value is $285,000 at year 10 and $712,000 at year 20 per the contract's own illustration.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:JointSurvivorshipPolicy. Article V §5.2: 'Projected cash value: End of Year 10: $285,000. End of Year 20: $712,000.'

contract_responses.details: This return of premium term policy explicitly does not build cash value for investment growth. The only recoverable value is the return of premiums at end of term. Policy loans are not permitted.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:ReturnOfPremiumTermPolicy. Policy Summary: 'Unique: no loans/withdrawals.' Section 3: ROP benefit is forfeited on lapse.

contract_responses.details: This is an indexed universal life policy that credits interest based on an index with a 0% floor and 10.5% S&P 500 cap — not through direct market sub-accounts with M&E charges. It is not a VUL product and this charge framework does not apply. The IUL uses a 3.5% premium load and $12/month admin charge but has fundamentally different crediting mechanics.
contract_responses.source: Source defines this as li:IndexedUniversalLifePolicy. Article 4.4: 'premiumLoadPercentage 3.5.' Article 5.2: 'Administrative Charge: $12 per month.' Article 2.1A: S&P 500 cap 10.5%, floor 0%.
